notes: Ran through a time challenge and everything was fine.
title: Redout: Enhanced Edition

notes: Tested with a Steam Controller and an Xbox 360 controller. When one of my controllers ran out of power I had to restart the game to make it detect it again, that's not much of an issue though. Local multiplayer works. The game may stutter a bit for the first few minutes, probably due to shaders compiling. After that it's perfectly smooth.
title: Redout: Enhanced Edition

notes: For the first minute or so after loading a level, it runs fine but freezes up every so often. Some levels can cause severe freezing for a small period. After a few minutes it runs perfectly.
title: Redout: Enhanced Edition

cpu: AMD A8-3850 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: On first start, it does not seem to have installed all the 3rd party libraries = choppy framerate. After restarting it, all ran smooth and fine in 60FPS. Alt+Enter works for fullscreen toggle. UE4 based game has plenty of graphic setting options, which may impact your performance, depending also on your hardware. Not necessarily a WINE/Proton thing.
title: Redout: Enhanced Edition

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Unlike my system with a Geforce 1050 TI, this game runs at 30fps with Epic settings enabled. If you set the resolution quality to 80% or set all settings down to "High", you get a 60fps experience with onboard graphics in UE4.

notes:
This game has really come a long way, performance-wise since when proton first came out.
Some minor stuttering on the first couple races and on the results screen, but after that... wowweee!!!  Blazing speed and buttery smooth game play at 1080p near all max settings.  I disabled E-Sync but at the end of the day I'm not entirely sure that was even necessary.

notes: Runs perfectt out of the box, except it runs better with opengl than vulkan. 10/10

notes: The game usually freezes when loading a map. I have only gotten into a race once and the game crashed within 30 seconds, but ran very smoothly beforehand. Disabling Esync, Disabling D3d11, and using Wind D3d11 were all tried, but made no difference.

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2500U with Radeon Vega Mobile Gfx
notes: Runs great out of the box.
title: Redout: Enhanced Edition

notes:
Using Mesa-git on my Radeon Vii, the game runs flawlessly most of the time.
Only problems i noticed were slight to intense stuttering during first ten seconds, sometimes for slightly longer than the hole first lap. This seems to be dependend on the map chosen as well as the time played since starting the game, with the problem dissipating after a short while. The two maps I tested on Volcano, had a lot of visual glitches to the point of being unplayable though. Performance seems otherwise on par with windows, running in 4K@60hz.
title: Redout: Enhanced Edition

notes:
Slow game.
A little better with Disable Esync and Use WineD3d but still slow.
Crash with all settings high.
title: Redout: Enhanced Edition

notes:
there is a noticeable drop in FPS from windows, about 15-20 fps lower (100-85), however the game FEELS smoother and faster none the less.

I found that disabling particles and portal effects entirely allowed me to keep every other setting at max, 100% resolution at 1440p. if you don't disabled both of these the fps drops to 50-60, so my guess is that there's something in the emulation that just doesn't like these effects. 

as other users reported, the game does stutter a little bit at first, so its best to do a little test run before playing a serious race the first time you start the game. aside from that it runs very smoothly.
title: Redout: Enhanced Edition

notes: Runs fine but it seems that the blur effects and other advanced graphical options are more taxing than under windows. Still perfectly playable.
